data.description:
# Provider-generic agent guidance: AGENTS.md migration, doc de-dup, client template, portable skills

## Context

Our agent guidance is Claude-locked and bloated. `CLAUDE.md` is 665 lines and duplicates
large parts of `docs/` (an established anti-pattern — the file loads every session, and
past a threshold it degrades instruction-following). `CLAUDE.local.md` mixes genuinely
machine/operator-local content with generally-applicable project rules copied from
`CLAUDE.md`. And the guidance is readable only by Claude Code, while `AGENTS.md` is now the
de-facto cross-vendor standard (OpenAI 2025 → donated to the Linux Foundation's Agentic AI
Foundation, co-founded by Anthropic/OpenAI/Block, Dec 2025) read natively by Codex, Cursor,
Copilot's coding agent, Zed, Windsurf, Amp, Factory, Jules, Devin, RooCode, and Cline.

The repo is already ~30% migrated (`.agents/rules/rebar.md`, `.agents/mcp_config.json`,
`.codex/config.toml` with a "migrate CLAUDE.md" shim comment, `docs/session-id-shims.md`,
and `examples/claude-skills/` reframed as retargetable). This epic finishes that migration.

**Locked decisions (design session):** (1) `AGENTS.md` becomes the canonical root file; a
thin `CLAUDE.md` bridges Claude Code via a one-line `@AGENTS.md` import; the duplicated bulk
is reduced to one-line pointers into `docs/`. (2) A shipped `templates/AGENTS.md` carries the
portable "how to drive rebar" guidance for client projects, kept in sync with the authoritative
docs by a test. (3) Example skills stay in the SKILL.md format (now a cross-provider open
standard) but are de-branded and generalized. See the design-of-record session log for the
full research basis (AGENTS.md governance, provider matrix, OSS patterns, anti-patterns).

**Key insight:** "CLAUDE.md duplicates docs" and "separate rebar's guide from a client
template" are the same fix — push the portable rebar-usage guidance into `docs/` as the single
source of truth, and have thin, audience-specific files point at it. rebar's own `AGENTS.md`
keeps only rebar-internal dev specifics inline (Gerrit split-remote, module-size policy,
Serena, local-dev-env, worktree/venv, CI-before-close, signing).

## Success Criteria

- Any supported harness (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, …) picks up rebar's dev guidance from a
  single canonical source without per-provider content duplication.
- The root guidance file is lean (target ≤ ~200 lines) and duplicates no doc that already has
  an authoritative version.
- A client project adopting rebar can copy one provider-neutral template to teach its own
  agents to drive rebar, with no rebar-internal specifics leaking in.
- No guidance is lost in the move — every rule removed from `CLAUDE.md`/`CLAUDE.local.md` has
  an authoritative home in `AGENTS.md` or `docs/`.

## Scope

In: the root guidance files, `docs/` homes for CLAUDE.md-native content, the `.codex`/`.agents`
shims, `examples/` skills, a client template + drift test, and a repo-wide reference sweep.
Out: changing rebar's runtime behavior, the Gerrit/CI landing flow itself, or Jira sync.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All child stories are closed and their changes are landed on `main`.
- [ ] `AGENTS.md` exists at the repo root and is the canonical guidance file; `CLAUDE.md` is a
  thin `@AGENTS.md` pointer (plus only Claude-only notes).
  - Proving: `test -f AGENTS.md && rg -c '@AGENTS.md' CLAUDE.md`
- [ ] `templates/AGENTS.md` (client-usable, provider-neutral) exists and a drift-guard test
  keeps it in sync with the authoritative source.
  - Proving: `test -f templates/AGENTS.md && make test`
- [ ] The example skills directory is provider-neutral (renamed from `examples/claude-skills`)
  and `make lint && make test` pass on the merged result.
  - Proving: `rg -l 'examples/claude-skills' . | wc -l` returns 0
data.title: Provider-generic agent guidance: AGENTS.md migration, doc de-dup, client template, portable skills
